task:
<command-message>superpowers:requesting-code-review</command-message>
<command-name>/superpowers:requesting-code-review</command-name>
<command-args>Please review the changes in this branch. Compare them to origin/main to figure out what changed.</command-args>

What can we do to address 1? In regard to 2: I tried to keep those comments around but it looks like `mise lint` removes them every time it's run.

Regarding measure: Are there any good cases where it would simplify the code in the current codebase? Otherwise, remove it.
user_feedback_text:
What can we do to address 1? In regard to 2: I tried to keep those comments around but it looks like `mise lint` removes them every time it's run.

Regarding measure: Are there any good cases where it would simplify the code in the current codebase? Otherwise, remove it.

task: The span name write_session_files is misleading here: this block only loads session state and generates the commit message; the actual file writing happens later (inside SaveStep) or elsewhere. Consider renaming the span to reflect the work being timed (e.g., commit message generation) or expanding the span to cover the actual session file writes if that’s what you intend to measure.
user_feedback_text: The span name write_session_files is misleading here: this block only loads session state and generates the commit message; the actual file writing happens later (inside SaveStep) or elsewhere. Consider renaming the span to reflect the work being timed (e.g., commit message generation) or expanding the span to cover the actual session file writes if that’s what you intend to measure.

task: Span.End() adds slog.String("component", "perf") as a caller-provided attr while logging.Debug also extracts component from the context. This will emit duplicate component keys in the JSON log line (e.g., one from context like "hooks" plus "perf"), which can confuse log ingestion/parsing. Consider setting the component via context (e.g., logging.WithComponent(s.ctx, "perf")) and not emitting a separate component attribute, or otherwise ensure only one component key is logged.
user_feedback_text: Span.End() adds slog.String("component", "perf") as a caller-provided attr while logging.Debug also extracts component from the context. This will emit duplicate component keys in the JSON log line (e.g., one from context like "hooks" plus "perf"), which can confuse log ingestion/parsing. Consider setting the component via context (e.g., logging.WithComponent(s.ctx, "perf")) and not emitting a separate component attribute, or otherwise ensure only one component key is logged.

task: Can you pull in main and fix any merge conflicts?
user_feedback_text: Can you pull in main and fix any merge conflicts?
